creator: derwood@naebunny.net
text:
Controller card came with SansDigital TR8M Enclosure.  There are four
Seageate ST2000DL003-9VT166 drives in the enclosure.

Speeds are never over 1MB/sec when there is a partition on the drive.

Writes to the raw device are in excess of 100MB/sec.  As soon as the
drive is partitioned, writes drop down to 1MB/sec.  Exchanging the
HighPoint RocketRaid 622 for a Silicon Image 3132 increases single drive
speeds back up to 126 MB/sec.

--------------------------------------------------------------

------------------------------------------------------------

/boot/loader.conf
ahci_load="YES"
geom_stripe_load="YES"
vfs.zfs.prefetch_disable=1
vm.kmem_size="330M"
vm.kmem_size_max="340M"
vfs.zfs.arc_max="40M"
vfs.zfs.vdev.cache.size="6M"

Kernel config is 100% stock.  Zero changes.

/etc/sysctl.conf
# $FreeBSD: src/etc/sysctl.conf,v 1.8.40.1 2011/09/23 00:51:37 kensmith Exp $
#
#  This file is read when going to multi-user and its contents piped thru
#  ``sysctl'' to adjust kernel values.  ``man 5 sysctl.conf'' for details.
#

# Uncomment this to prevent users from seeing information about processes that
# are being run under another UID.
#security.bsd.see_other_uids=0

How-To-Repeat: alexandria# gpart create -s gpt ada7
ada7 created
alexandria# gpart add -t freebsd-ufs -b 1M -a 4k ada7
ada7p1 added
alexandria# newfs -U -f 4096 /dev/ada7p1
Reduced frags per cylinder group from 189440 to 189432 to enlarge last cyl group
/dev/ada7p1: 1907728.1MB (3907027080 sectors) block size 32768, fragment size 4096
        using 2579 cylinder groups of 739.97MB, 23679 blks, 47360 inodes.
        with soft updates


alexandria# gpart show ada7
